case.title: Police Academy
case.subtitle: Learn how to investigate, unlock clues, and solve a case.

finalQuestions.label: Why did the envelope get moved?
options: To hide that Miles returned to recover his misplaced keycard and avoid blame
It was moved by mistake during cleaning

finalQuestions.label: How did you deduce the truth?
options: The keycard, cleanup timing, and Rosa's statement contradicted Miles's story
Rosa had the most access to the room

finalQuestions.label: What is the strongest proof clue?

scene_001_opening.title: Welcome to Police Academy
scene_001_opening.text:
Sergeant Vale slides a sealed briefing envelope onto the table, then frowns.

'The envelope was here ten minutes ago. Now it isn't. Good. Training starts now.'

This mini case teaches you how to use the tabs, unlock clues, and complete checkpoints. Your job is to figure out who moved the envelope and why.
choices: Go to Interviews and question Rosa Bell

scene_002_briefing_table.title: Briefing Table
scene_002_briefing_table.text: A faint coffee ring sits near the missing envelope marker. Someone stood at the table after the briefing ended.
choices: Go to Interviews and question Rosa Bell

scene_003_seating_area.title: Seating Area
scene_003_seating_area.text:
Under a chair leg, you find a misplaced trainee keycard. Sergeant Vale nods.

'Good. Physical evidence matters more than body language.'
choices: Go to Interviews and question Rosa Bell

scene_004_access_board.title: Room Access Board
scene_004_access_board.text:
The board lists who had access after briefing: Evan Pike, Rosa Bell, and Miles Trent.

Sergeant Vale reminds you that access creates suspicion, not proof.

scene_005_round1_checkpoint.title: Round 1 Checkpoint
scene_005_round1_checkpoint.prompt: What is the most important lesson from the first round?
answers: The most nervous suspect is always guilty
Every unlocked clue should appear in the Clues tab
Janitors are always the best witnesses
scene_005_round1_checkpoint.correct: Correct. Scene actions and interview topics should push evidence into the Clues tab so you can review what you have actually confirmed.
scene_005_round1_checkpoint.incorrect: Not quite. The key lesson here is that your unlocked evidence should appear in the Clues tab so you can reason from confirmed information, not guesswork.

scene_006_round2_brief.title: Round 2 Brief
scene_006_round2_brief.text:
Sergeant Vale folds his arms.

'Now stop chasing vibes. Compare stories to evidence. That's deduction.'

Round 2 teaches contradiction: who said something that the clues cannot support?
choices: Go to Interviews and question Evan Pike
Go to Interviews and question Miles Trent
Go to Interviews and question Rosa Bell again

scene_007_log_review.title: Briefing Log
scene_007_log_review.text: The sign-out record shows the envelope was still logged at dismissal and disappeared after the official session ended.

scene_008_round2_checkpoint.title: Round 2 Checkpoint
scene_008_round2_checkpoint.prompt: Which suspect's story is contradicted by the evidence?
scene_008_round2_checkpoint.correct: Correct. Miles claimed he never re-entered the room, but the keycard, timestamp, and Rosa's follow-up statement do not support that.
scene_008_round2_checkpoint.incorrect: Not quite. The contradiction is in Miles Trent's version of events, not just in who seemed the most suspicious.

scene_013_final_accusation.title: Final Accusation
scene_013_final_accusation.text: Sergeant Vale taps the table. 'Now prove you can finish a case the right way. Name who moved it, why, and what proves it.'

evidence_coffee_ring.title: Coffee Cup Ring
evidence_coffee_ring.desc: A fresh ring on the briefing table shows someone stood at the envelope position after the session ended.

evidence_cleaning_note.title: Cleaning Shift Note
evidence_cleaning_note.desc: Rosa's cleanup note shows cleaning started later than Miles implied.

evidence_keycard.title: Keycard Under Chair
evidence_keycard.desc: A trainee keycard was found under a chair, suggesting someone returned to recover something.

evidence_log_timestamp.title: Briefing Log Timestamp
evidence_log_timestamp.desc: The envelope disappeared after official dismissal, not during the briefing itself.

evidence_rosa_followup.title: Rosa's Follow-Up Statement
evidence_rosa_followup.desc: Rosa saw Miles tuck the envelope into a side drawer after coming back for his keycard.

suspects.name: Evan Pike
suspects.public_read: Nervous, eager, and easy to rattle. He looks guilty fast, but nerves are not evidence.

suspects.name: Rosa Bell
suspects.public_read: Calm, observant, and easy to overlook. She saw more than she first admitted.

suspects.name: Miles Trent
suspects.public_read: Confident and polished, but defensive when his timeline is challenged.

public.text: Sergeant Vale begins the training investigation.

rosa_bell.name: Rosa Bell
rosa_bell.intro: Rosa leans on her mop handle. 'I clean up what people leave behind. Sometimes that includes the truth.'

topics.label: Cleanup Timing
topics.response: Cleanup did not start right away. The room sat open for a few minutes after dismissal.

topics.label: What did you really see?
topics.response: Miles came back in, found his keycard was missing, and then shoved the envelope into the side drawer like he wanted the room to look untouched.

evan_pike.name: Evan Pike
evan_pike.intro: Evan straightens up too quickly. 'I know I look bad. I just didn't want to be the first person blamed.'

topics.label: Why were you near the table?
topics.response: I moved my coffee and got out of the way. That's it. I never touched the envelope.

topics.label: Did you lose a keycard?
topics.response: No. Mine never left my pocket. Ask Miles if you want to talk about missing things.

miles_trent.name: Miles Trent
miles_trent.intro: Miles keeps his tone even. 'I left when everyone else did. If the envelope vanished later, that wasn't me.'

topics.label: Your Timeline
topics.response: Briefing ended, I walked out, and I did not come back.

topics.label: Missing Keycard
topics.response: That could be anyone's keycard. Finding it doesn't prove I came back.

hints.1: To unlock the first checkpoint, inspect both physical areas and interview Rosa at least once.
hints.2: Round 2 is about contradiction. Compare what Miles says to the keycard, timestamp, and Rosa's follow-up statement.
